chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI PRESENT: Mr. Justice Aqeel Ahmed Abbasi, ACJ Mr. Justice Abdul Mobeen Lakho, J C.P. No.D-5930 of 2023 ___________________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature of Judge ___________________________________________________________________ FRESH CASE: 1. For order on CMA No.27649/2022 (Urgent). 2. For order on office objection No.19. 3. For order on CMA No.27650/2022 (Exemption). 4. For order on CMA No.27651/2022 (Stay). 5. For hearing of main case. ----------- Dated; 11th December 2023 Petitioner Muhammad Rizwan present in person. -*-*-*-*-*- 1. Urgency granted. 2. Petitioner undertakes to comply with office objection before the next date of hearing. 3. Exemption granted subject to all just exceptions. 4&5. After hearing the petitioner at some length and from perusal of the record, it appears that the petitioner has expressed his grievance against some decisions taken by the Executive Committee of Management (ECM) of Pakistan Steel Mills dated 02.11.2023, whereby, according to petitioner, he and other 95 retrenched employees, whose contract period was extended upto 16.12.2023, have been cancelled/terminated and the matter has been referred to the Board of Directors for its rectification/approval, which shows that there is no final order so far with regard to the employment of the petitioner and other retrenched employees, whereas, instant petition would otherwise not be maintainable in respect of Pakistan Steel Mills, as the petitioner has the remedy to approach NIRC against the impugned order. Petitioner will not press instant petition at this stage

After hearing the petitioner at some length and from perusal of the record, it appears that the petitioner has expressed his grievance against some decisions taken by the Executive Committee of Management (ECM) of Pakistan Steel Mills dated 02.11.2023, whereby, according to petitioner, he and other 95 retrenched employees, whose contract period was extended upto 16.12.2023, have been cancelled/terminated and the matter has been referred to the Board of Directors for its rectification/approval, which shows that there is no final order so far with regard to the employment of the petitioner and other retrenched employees, whereas, instant petition would otherwise not be maintainable in respect of Pakistan Steel Mills, as the petitioner has the remedy to approach NIRC against the impugned order. Petitioner will not press instant petition at this stage [Page 2] and will seek the remedy by approaching the Board of Directors and in case of any adverse order, will seek redressal of his grievance by approaching the relevant forum in accordance with law. Instant Constitutional Petition stands disposed of in the above terms. ACTING CHIEF JUSTICE J U D G E *Farhan/PS*
